_comment: Source of truth for BaaS service tiers. The control plane embeds a byte-identical copy at go/control-plane/internal/packages/packages.json (m28 asserts they match). 'capabilities' is a NARROWING mask: a false flag removes a capability the engine otherwise has; the Rust planner (apply_capability_overrides) can never widen past the engine descriptor. 'engines' gates mount registration. 'limits' feeds the per-tenant token bucket. 'pool_policy.max_mounts' caps how many databases a tenant may register. Addons map 1:1 to compose planes.
_measured: v2 rps/burst are derived from bench-capacity (artifacts/bench/capacity-essential.json, 2026-06-11): a single mount sustains ~400 rps of reads at p95 < 2 ms before the connection-pool cliff (500+ rps → p99 10s + 5xx). Advertised rps = floor(measured_ceiling × fair_share × 0.5), burst = 2×rps, so a single tenant never alone saturates its pool. max's 800 rps assumes the per-tier DATA_PLANE_MAX_POOLS policy + supavisor multiplexing (B4); horizontal scale lifts it further. See wiki/offer-sheet-v2.md.
_aliases_comment: free→nano (the cheapest shape — v1 mapped free onto essential, the ~$13/mo tier, which was upside down). enterprise→max. 'pro'/'basic'/'essential' resolve directly. An additive migration widens the tenants.plan CHECK so nano/basic become assignable.

nano.label: Nano
nano._comment: The single-binary PocketBase-class edition (binocle-nano): embedded SQLite, in-process auth, ~5.1 MB image / ~2.0 MiB idle (artifacts/nano-vs-pocketbase.json). Ships CRUD + graph + field masks in one binary. The free-tier shape.
nano._tenancy_guidance: One app per binary. Graduates to basic with zero rewrites (same SDK, same codebase).

basic.label: Basic
basic._comment: Pi-class lean tier (≤512MB): SQLite-first, served Node-free through the Rust /data/v1 bypass (Makefile PACKAGE_basic := go rust). CRUD only — scale to essential for aggregate + the Node orchestration features (graph, masks, automations).
basic._tenancy_guidance: A private single-app BaaS on a Pi or $5 VPS. rps 100 is well under the measured ~400 rps single-pool read ceiling.

essential.label: Essential
essential._comment: Full-feature single product under 1 GB. Differs from basic by a real capability — aggregate (count/sum/group-by) — not just a rate, served by the Node orchestration plane it carries.
essential._tenancy_guidance: One full-feature product. rps 200 = half the measured single-pool read ceiling, leaving headroom for the write path (insert p99 583 ms — the outbox tail, tracked D-write-tail).

pro.label: Pro
pro._comment: Multi-engine SaaS with realtime + storage. Adds batch + transactions and 5 more engines.
pro._tenancy_guidance: A multi-engine SaaS. rps 400 = the measured single-pool read ceiling; amortized across a shared host this is < $1/tenant (see m39 multitenant artifact).

max.label: Max
max._comment: Multi-tenant platform. Every engine + every capability incl. DDL. rps 800 requires the per-tier DATA_PLANE_MAX_POOLS policy + supavisor connection multiplexing (B4) to sustain past the single-pool 400 rps cliff — horizontal scale lifts it further. max_mounts 50 is bounded by that pool policy so a tenant's quota can never exceed what the plane holds.
max._tenancy_guidance: A multi-tenant cloud platform. The 10K-tenant validation (make verify-m39 SCALE=10000) backs the density story.
